_meta.description: Cycle & innings scoring for all 11 commodity verticals. Innings 1=very early, 9=very late. Scores 0-100.

cycles.sum: Year 1-2 of Western supply chain build — now with China actively escalating. In 2025 China expanded export controls from processing technology to HREE oxides, metals, and alloys, directly threatening Western magnet supply chains for defense and EVs. MP Fort Worth magnet facility in commercial ramp — first OEM-qualified US NdFeB at production scale, with GM OEM agreement active. Lynas Seadrift TX DoD-funded HREE separation facility nearing completion (2026 target) — still the only meaningful HREE separation project outside China. HREE (Dy, Tb) separation: still essentially zero Western commercial capacity. Robotics demand multiplier now entering analyst models: AI-enabled automation and manufacturing reshoring together are creating new REE motor demand not yet in commodity demand forecasts. Low bc risk — defense procurement is policy-driven, not economically discretionary.

Year 2-3 of decade-long structural rebuild. Russian enrichment import ban (2024) forcing Western supply chain rebuild — year 1-2 of that process. AI PPA buildout has accelerated far beyond the CEG-Microsoft TMI template: Amazon/Dominion, Google/Kairos Power SMR fleet, and other hyperscalers now signed or in active negotiation for nuclear baseload. Nuclear is now the preferred 24/7 carbon-free baseload for hyperscalers globally — price certainty (15-20yr contracts at $110-130/MWh) plus zero-carbon plus 90%+ capacity factor is unmatched by any alternative. Centrus Piketon HALEU production progressing. SPUT building structural physical inventory position. The structural deficit has not resolved — spot price volatility is noise against a decade-long supply rebuild.

Year 3-4 of a 10-year supercycle that is accelerating, not plateauing. The numbers are now in earnings. PWR Q1 2026 backlog: $48.5B (record; +$20B in two years). ETN Q1 2026: 228 GW of data center backlog = 12 years of orders at current build rates; DC equipment orders +240% YoY; Electrical Americas orders +42% YoY. GEV Q1 2026 orders: $18.3B (+71% organically); gas turbine slot reservations raised to 110 GW target; total backlog heading to $200B by 2027 (pulled forward one year from 2028). Transformer lead times remain 2-4yr — no new manufacturing capacity has relieved the bottleneck. Nuclear AI PPAs restructuring power contracting fundamentals: Amazon/Dominion 1.92 GW, Google/Kairos SMR, Meta Prometheus 6.6 GW all signed or in late negotiation. This is the clearest evidence-based supercycle across the entire hard assets universe.

TC/RC confirmed negative across 2024-2025 — multiple smelters globally reported negative treatment charges, validating the structural concentrate shortage. Critical supply revision: FCX Grasberg Block Cave geometry issue cuts ~9% of copper and ~7% of gold output through 2031 — a multi-year reduction from the world's largest mine, not priced by consensus. SCCO Tia Maria: Peru revoked the production authorization in March 2026, then reinstated it 32 days later — LatAm political risk is real and must be modeled, but the project is back on track for mid-2027 start. No major new greenfield copper mine has reached FID. S&P Global 8Mt/yr deficit by 2030 thesis gaining mainstream credibility. China construction demand weakness offset by grid, EV, and data center copper demand. Leaching technology adds incremental supply — FCX targeting 300-400M lbs/yr by 2026-27 from existing deposits.

Year 2 of scarcity recognition. Water constrains Atacama copper/lithium production, nuclear SMR siting, and AI data center permitting in Arizona. Energy Recovery (ERII) desal near-monopoly is the cleanest listed expression at the water scarcity intersection. ARIS Water (Permian produced water) is most direct O&G water play. Low bc risk — mines need water regardless of economic conditions.

Structural thesis reinforced by a geopolitical shock. Iran struck Qatar's Ras Laffan LNG complex on March 18, 2026 — approximately 17% of Qatar's LNG capacity damaged, with a 3-5yr repair timeline. EU gas storage fell to ~35% in late May 2026 vs. 48% five-year average. US became geopolitically irreplaceable: record US LNG exports 11.7M tonnes in March 2026 with ~64% to Europe; Cheniere was upgraded across Wall Street as Qatar supply evaporated. Cheniere 2025 record: $19.976B revenue, >46M tonnes delivered. Corpus Christi Stage 3 Train 5 achieved first LNG in February 2026; Trains 6-7 on track. The 20yr European SPAs were already permanent — the Qatar disruption makes them existentially necessary. AI data center power demand (gas-fired CCGT) is the third structural driver. Gas is simultaneously a NATO foreign policy asset and an AI infrastructure input.

Linde and APD are Western-controlled strategic assets with geopolitical tailwind and no geopolitical risk. On-site plant model has near-infinite switching cost and 15-20yr contracted revenue. ATI is mid-cycle on Ti supply chain diversification from Russia. Materion (MTRN) is underowned at <$1.5B market cap with essentially no listed competition for US beryllium — defense-critical.

Export controls are now embedded market reality, not an emerging catalyst. Ga/Ge controls (Aug 2023) and Sb controls (Sept 2024) have permanently repriced affected markets: antimony spiked from ~$12,000/tonne pre-controls to ~$57,800/tonne peak by April 2025, now holding ~$51,800/tonne — a 4x structural reprice. Perpetua Resources (PPTA) crossed from permitting to construction: early works commenced October 2025, $2.7B EXIM Bank loan approved, DoD funding >$80M committed. At current gold prices (~$4,500+/oz), Stibnite NPV sensitivity reaches $6.1B — this is no longer a speculative junior miner. Alphamin (AFMJF) record FY2025 tin production 18,576 tonnes; 2026 guidance ~20,000 tonnes; LME tin ~$49,663/MT (+55% YoY), deficit projected to tighten. DoD DPA Title III and Project Vault ($12B) starting to deploy capital — Western policy response moved from zero to nascent but remains severely lagging the strategic threat.

Cycle turn confirmed. Li carbonate (LCE) troughed at ~$8,000/tonne mid-2025 (down ~83% from 2022 peak) and has recovered to ~$21,000/tonne by May 2026 (+162% from trough). ALB Q1 2026 adjusted EBITDA +148% YoY — the recovery is now showing in earnings, not just spot price. SQM's single largest risk resolved: Atacama operating rights extended to 2060 via Codelco joint venture deal (eliminating the nationalization overhang). 2026-2030 demand wave thesis unchanged: global EV penetration accelerating, grid-scale BESS now growing independently of EV cycle (standalone economics work). Stationary storage demand = the demand driver consensus is still underweighting. IRA domestic content requirements create US reshoring incentive with a 5-10yr supply response lag — patience still required but the trough is behind us.

Post-2022 spike normalization. Potash prices retreated from highs. Phosphate is the long-duration structural thesis — non-renewable, Morocco concentration, rising demand. Nitrogen highly correlated to natural gas prices. Nutrien (NTR) trades at a discount to intrinsic strategic value as the only complete NPK company globally.

Offshore drilling mid-cycle recovery (UDW utilization ~90%+, dayrates rising from 2020 trough). Shale returns-focused with capital discipline maintained. Refining structurally tighter post-2020 closures. Tankers have Russian rerouting structural tailwind. Long-term oil demand plateauing in developed markets. Best expressed via offshore (TDW, RIG/VAL UDW), complex refiners (VLO), product tankers (STNG).

Gold ATH: $5,589/oz on January 28, 2026 — highest ever recorded. Pulled back to ~$4,523/oz by May 2026 but J.P. Morgan projects $5,000/oz by Q4 2026; central bank net purchases Q1 2026: 244 tonnes. De-dollarization thesis intact and accelerating. Streaming company FCF at these prices is exceptional: FNV 2025 record revenue $1.82B, net income $1.11B (2x YoY); WPM now world's largest precious metals streamer by market cap with 2026 GEO guidance 860K-940K; RGLD acquired Sandstorm Gold ($3.5B, October 2025) transforming into a large-cap streaming/royalty house in a single transaction. The streaming structure maximizes FCF leverage at peak gold prices: fixed-cost streams + full commodity upside. Palladium in structural ICE-to-EV demand decline — avoid. Platinum hydrogen economy demand long-dated (2028-2035). SA structural fragility (Eskom, labor, grade decline) persistent. Best expressed: FNV, WPM, RGLD — exceptional FCF generation with zero SA operational risk.
